commands.repair_reason: Human maintainer review is needed because the linked bug is already fixed and the remaining alias changes a security-sensitive hook contract.; Needs attention: The patch changes a security-sensitive hook payload and can route code-mode cells through existing exec/command authorization logic without a distinct discriminator.; Review finding: [P1] Do not expose code-mode cells as generic exec commands yet — src/agents... (sha=5c7c872e0be90a0bb0ad55b4bf7c160ed2c3e889)
commands.review_summary:
The PR threads hook context into Pi custom-tool conversion, adds a code-mode `exec` `command` alias with code/command reconciliation, updates tests, and documents the alias.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blocker at source level: the PR mirrors code-mode `exec` into `params.command` while keeping toolName `exec`. The original missing-context bug no longer reproduces on current main because the merged maintainer fix passes `toolHookContext`.
commands.review_followup: Human maintainer review is needed because the linked bug is already fixed and the remaining alias changes a security-sensitive hook contract.

target.title: fix(code-mode): pass hook context to outer exec
target.body:
Fixes #83387.

## Problem

When code-mode exposes its outer `exec` control tool through the Pi custom-tool adapter, `before_tool_call` runs without the active run hook context. Hook handlers and trusted policies therefore cannot reliably identify the agent, session, run, or tool call for that outer dispatch.

The outer code-mode `exec` input also used `code`, while conventional exec-style hook policies usually inspect `command`. That made it harder for a single policy to reason about the outer code-mode dispatch and normal exec-shaped dispatches consistently.

## Change and value

This PR threads the active hook context into the Pi custom-tool adapter for the outer dispatch path, so `before_tool_call` receives the same agent/session/run/tool-call identity that cataloged inner tools already receive.

It also accepts `command` as an exec-compatible alias for code-mode `exec` while keeping the model-facing `code` field compatible. Hook payloads mirror the two aliases for code-mode `exec` only, reject divergent aliases before execution, and treat non-string counterpart values as absent so policies still see the executable source under both expected names.

## Who is affected, and who is not

Affected:
- code-mode users with `before_tool_call` hooks or trusted policies that depend on agent/session/run identity
- policy authors who want outer code-mode `exec` to have an exec-compatible `command` field

Not affected:
- normal shell `exec` behavior
- non-code-mode tools named `exec`
- the existing code-mode `code` field, which remains accepted

## Why now

This closes a policy/context gap at the boundary where code-mode is dispatched as an outer tool call. The fix keeps the behavior scoped to marked code-mode control tools and adds regression coverage for both unwrapped adapter-owned hooks and wrapper-owned hooks.

## Implementation

- Pass `catalogToolHookContext` into `splitSdkTools()` from `runEmbeddedAttempt()`.
- Thread optional `HookContext` through `splitSdkTools()` and `toToolDefinitions()`.
- Add a small code-mode control-tool helper for marking control tools and normalizing/reconciling `code`/`command` hook params.
- Apply the code-mode exec alias normalization in both the adapter-owned hook path and the `wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook()` path.
- Reject divergent `code` and `command` values in code-mode `exec` before execution.
- Document the alias contract in `docs/reference/code-mode.md`.

## Risks

The hook event shape for code-mode `exec` now includes a mirrored alias field when callers provide only one of `code` or `command`. That is intentional so existing `code` policies and exec-style `command` policies can both inspect the executable source, but policy authors should still key on `toolName` before applying shell-specific `command` checks.

## Verification

- Local: `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/code-mode-control-tools.ts src/agents/code-mode.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts src/agents/pi-tool-definition-adapter.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/tool-split.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts docs/reference/code-mode.md`
- Local: `git diff --check`
- Local: direct `node --import tsx` behavior probe confirmed `agentId=main`, mirrored `code`/`command` for code-only and command-only calls, and divergent aliases rejected.
- Local: `timeout 120s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts -t "outer code-mode exec"`
- Remote GCP Spot, `us-east1-b`, `e2-standard-4`, Node `22.22.2`, pnpm `11.1.0`: install, format check, `git diff --check`, targeted code-mode alias tests, targeted outer code-mode hook integration tests, and `pnpm build` all passed.
- Review: `timeout 900s codex review --uncommitted` reported no discrete correctness issues.
- Review: Claude diff review reported no P0/P1 findings; remaining items were P2 documentation/ownership suggestions.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Outer code-mode `exec` dispatches now provide `before_tool_call` with active agent/session/run/tool-call context and an exec-compatible `command` alias while keeping `code` compatible.

**Real environment tested:** Local worktree plus remote GCP Spot VM in `us-east1-b` using `e2-standard-4`, Node `22.22.2`, pnpm `11.1.0`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Local direct probe invoked the Pi custom-tool definition for code-mode `exec` with code-only, command-only, and divergent-alias inputs. Remote validation ran install, format check, `git diff --check`, targeted Vitest lanes, and `pnpm build` on the pushed patch contents.

**Evidence after fix:** Hook events for outer code-mode `exec` included `ctx.agentId = "main"`, `sessionKey`, `sessionId`, `runId`, and `toolCallId`. Code-only and command-only inputs both arrived at the hook with matching `code` and `command` fields. `{ code: "return 1;", command: "return 2;" }` was rejected before execution.

**Observed result after fix:** No missing hook context was observed for the outer code-mode `exec` path, alias mirroring was scoped to marked code-mode control tools, targeted tests passed, and the remote build passed.

**What was not tested:** The full code-mode test file was not rerun end-to-end in this PR because an existing hostile-loop timeout classification failure is being handled separately in #83444; this PR ran the new alias tests directly and the broader build successfully.
target.author: kaspre
